key_insight: An adaptive, foundation-model-assisted crystal structure prediction workflow can cut DFT labeling effort while still finding stable phases in complex alloys.
engineering_relevance: This approach makes high-throughput materials discovery more practical for multicomponent systems by reducing the cost of evolutionary CSP without sacrificing accuracy.
method: Start from a pretrained machine-learning interatomic potential.
Run evolutionary CSP to explore candidate crystal structures.
Iteratively select compact, representative structures for DFT labeling and fine-tune the model self-consistently.
evidence_signal: The workflow reproduces the known low-pressure convex hull for Ca--Fe--Ni and predicts a new stable compound, Ca6FeNi, above 100 GPa.
watch_item: Performance depends on how well the adaptive selection captures physically relevant configurations; poor sampling could miss important phases.
paper_title: Adaptive fine-tuning of foundation models for crystal structure prediction: Discovery of high-pressure phases in the CaFeNi system
